name: Arepas Paisas
one_liner: Colombian arepas on Speedwell's Latin row. The real deal.

name: Azure The Greek Mediterranean
one_liner: Greek-Mediterranean on Pine Street. BYOB. The blue-and-white room stands out in a town full of American menus.

name: Blue Morel
one_liner: Hotel restaurant inside the Westin Governor Morris. New American farm-to-table. Not where you go on a Tuesday — but worth it for a big occasion.

name: Chef Fredy's Table
one_liner: El Salvadoran. Personal menu. Everything cooked to order.

name: Coniglio's Old Fashioned
one_liner: ~200 pizzas a day. 2023 NJ Pizza Bowl winner. Cash only.

name: Cotto
one_liner: Opened December 2025. Ex-Michelin chef Jordan Petriello. Handmade pasta with imported Italian flour, house-churned gelato. BYOB. The most significant opening South Street has seen in years.

name: Dän Bakery & Café
one_liner: Central Asian pastry on South Street. The Honey Cake is why people line up. Handcrafted daily, European and Central Asian traditions.

name: El Encuentro Centro Americano
one_liner: Open at 5 AM. Honduran and Central American home cooking. Buffet-style, most entrées under $20, in business since 2003.

name: End of Elm
one_liner: The original Morristown gastropub. Craft cocktails, neighborhood crowd, happy hour that actually delivers.

name: Ethan and the Bean
one_liner: Non-profit café on Speedwell. Employs adults with developmental disabilities. Ethically sourced beans, outdoor tables.

name: Fig & Lily Garden
one_liner: BYOB Mediterranean with a garden patio. Cattano Ave, off the Green. Charcoal-grilled meats, hand-selected produce. One of the nicest outdoor dining spots in town.

name: Grasshopper Off the Green
one_liner: Three-story Irish pub on Morris Street. Live bands, karaoke, DJ. Four NJ locations. The place for a big group night out.

name: Ju-Ichi Japanese Restaurant
one_liner: The fuller Japanese menu down the block from Masago — bone broth ramen, udon, donburi, and BYOB.

name: Jungle Juice
one_liner: Made-to-order smoothies, juices, acai bowls. Morris Street. Family-owned since 2010. Three NJ locations.

name: Kirshenbaum Baking Co
one_liner: Paris-trained pastry chef, Schuyler Place. Croissants, cookies, breakfast sandwiches on house-baked bread. The Westfield location has been around longer but this one's yours.

name: Lin's Palace
one_liner: Family-run Taiwanese and Chinese on Speedwell. 40+ years. The Taiwanese sausage fried rice is what you come for.

name: Lokl Cafe
one_liner: Direct-trade coffee on South Street. Outdoor patio, handcrafted espresso. The kind of coffee shop South Street needed.

name: Marjan Fine Persian Grill
one_liner: Morristown's only Persian restaurant. Kebabs, stews, saffron rice.

name: Masago Japanese Cuisine
one_liner: BYOB sushi. The Friday night default for half the town.

name: Morristown Bagel and Deli
one_liner: Open since 1950. Same counter, same morning.

name: Morristown Diner
one_liner: Family-owned since 1994. Chrome, red booths, omelets, burgers. Open until 2 AM. The place that's always there.

name: Orale Mexican Kitchen
one_liner: Bold Mexican-American with strong daily-flavor frozen margs. The comps alone are worth the trip.

name: Parkside Tavern
one_liner: Opened October 2024. Old-world tavern inside HQ Plaza. Serious whiskey selection, craft cocktails. The newer sibling to Sushi Lounge.

name: Pavesi Ristorante
one_liner: Multi-level Italian on South Street. BYOB, no corkage fee. Heated patio, live guitar some nights. Homemade pasta, serious seafood.

name: Pierogies House
one_liner: Evelina makes everything herself. Her grandmother Julia's recipes.

name: Rinconcito Latino
one_liner: Pupusas, rice, beans, tostones. Elm Street. Home cooking, big portions, open early, costs nothing.

name: Rod's Steak & Seafood Grille
one_liner: Morristown's old-money steakhouse. Madison Hotel. Open since 1951. Tablecloths, proper cocktails, serious cuts.

name: Saigon Subs & Café
one_liner: The best sandwich in Morristown. Vietnamese banh mi and iced coffee so strong it'll rearrange your afternoon.

name: South + Pine American Eatery
one_liner: Leia Gaccione. Five Iron Chef appearances. BYOB patio, small room. One of the best reasons to live here.

name: Stirling Tavern
one_liner: American tavern at the south end of South Street. Full bar, specialty cocktails. Part of the Stirling Restaurant Group (same as Cotto).

name: Surreal Creamery
one_liner: Artisanal soft serve in mason jars. Washington Street. Matcha, taro, unique flavors. Bubble tea too.

name: Sushi Lounge
one_liner: Full bar, DJ nights, hibachi. HQ Plaza. Not your quiet neighborhood sushi — a night out. NJ Monthly 'Best Sushi' award.

name: Tacoria
one_liner: 11-location NJ chain but a real one — counter-service tacos, burritos, bowls. Full vegan menu. Fast, reliable, cheap.

name: Tatte Bakery & Café
one_liner: Boston chain. Airy room, good coffee, first-come seating. The pistachio latte is the thing.

name: The Address
one_liner: Greek-Mediterranean. BYOB. Heated patio. Book ahead.

name: The Committed Pig
one_liner: Award-winning brunch on the Green. Short rib/brisket burger blend. Decadent pancakes. Three NJ locations, this is the original.

name: The Frog
one_liner: Three-story bar on Washington Street. The Frog since 2024, Famished Frog before that. Billiards, shuffleboard, craft beer, gluten-free fryer. The 25-year Washington St anchor.

name: The Morris Proper
one_liner: Three or four visits in. Still finding things to order.

name: The Pastrami House
one_liner: Traditional NY deli on Speedwell. Opened March 2025. Loaded sandwiches, knishes, potato pancakes. Two NJ locations.

name: TOWN bar + kitchen
one_liner: Rooftop fireplace, Resy, globally inspired menu. 40NORTH group. Morristown's version of a destination restaurant that doesn't require a special occasion.
